The following browsers are supported: Chrome, Edge (v80 and later), Firefox and Safari."},{"url":"https://sports.yahoo.com/nfl/article/travis-kelce-told-chiefs-gm-after-week-18-lets-plan-on-me-being-here-for-2026-season-155740106.html","title":"Travis Kelce told Chiefs GM after Week 18 'let's plan on me being here' for 2026 season - Yahoo Sports","content":"Apparently, the decision to re-sign in Kansas City was a quick one for Kelce who, following Week 18, informed general manager Brett Veach that he was planning to return for the 2026 season.
In an interview with SiriusXM NFL radio on Wednesday, Veach said that within 24 hours of that final game against the Raiders he knew Kelce would be part of their plans for next season.
We came back here that Monday, we had player exit interviews and Travis popped into the office.
Kelce, who turns 37 in October, signed a one-year, $12 million deal with the Chiefs in March ahead of free agency.
Veach noted that knowing Kelce was in the fold was beneficial to their offseason planning.
The Chiefs finished 6-11 last season and missed the playoffs for the first time since 2014."}]}
